name: Nile
description: Longest river in Africa, flows northward through northeastern Africa.

name: Amazon
description: Largest river by discharge volume on Earth, flowing through the Amazon rainforest.

name: Yangtze
description: Longest river in Asia and China, flowing from the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau to the East China Sea.

name: Mississippi-Missouri
description: Longest river system in North America when combined, draining most of the continental United States.

name: Yenisei-Angara
description: Largest river system flowing to the Arctic Ocean, draining much of central Siberia.

name: Yellow River (Huang He)
description: Second longest river in China, known as the 'Mother River' and cradle of Chinese civilization.

name: Ob-Irtysh
description: One of the world's longest river systems, draining western Siberia.

name: Congo
description: Second longest river in Africa, the deepest river in the world, and largest by discharge in Africa.

name: Amur-Argun
description: Forms the border between the Russian Far East and northeastern China.

name: Lena
description: One of the longest rivers in the world, flowing through Siberia to the Arctic Ocean.

name: Mekong
description: Flows through six countries in Southeast Asia, supporting one of the world's most productive inland fisheries.

name: Mackenzie
source: Great Slave Lake / Finlay River headwaters
description: Longest river system in Canada, draining into the Arctic Ocean.

name: Niger
description: Third longest river in Africa, known for its unusual inner delta in Mali.

name: Murray-Darling
description: Longest river system in Australia, the lifeblood of the country's agricultural heartland.

name: Tocantins-Araguaia
description: Major river in Brazil flowing northward to join the Amazon estuary.

name: Volga
description: Longest river in Europe and Russia's most important waterway.

name: Indus
description: One of Asia's longest rivers, gave its name to India and ancient Indus Valley Civilization.

name: Yukon
description: Major river of the Yukon Territory and Alaska, historically important during the Klondike Gold Rush.

name: São Francisco
description: One of Brazil's most important rivers, entirely within Brazilian territory.

name: Danube
description: Second longest river in Europe, flowing through or bordering ten countries.

name: Brahmaputra
description: Major trans-boundary river flowing through Tibet, India, and Bangladesh.

name: Ganges
description: Sacred river of India, flowing through one of the world's most densely populated regions.

name: Euphrates
description: One of the two rivers defining Mesopotamia, cradle of ancient civilizations.

name: Zambezi
description: Fourth longest river in Africa, home to Victoria Falls, one of the world's largest waterfalls.

name: Nelson-Saskatchewan
description: Major river system in the Canadian prairies, draining into Hudson Bay.

name: Paraguay
source: Serra dos Parecis, Mato Grosso, Brazil
description: Main tributary of the Paraná River, flowing through the Pantanal wetland.

name: Kolyma
description: River in Siberia known for its extreme cold; the region was site of Soviet Gulag camps.

name: Amu Darya
description: Major Central Asian river, the ancient Oxus, forming part of the Afghanistan-Tajikistan border.

name: Columbia
description: Largest river draining into the Pacific Ocean from North America, major hydroelectric producer.

name: Colorado
source: La Poudre Pass, Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado
description: Carved the Grand Canyon; vital water source for the American Southwest and Mexico.
